macro_segments.title: Introduction to Daf 67 and Rabbi Zeira's Innovation Creating a Hybrid of Niddah and Zavah

micro_segments.title: Context: End of First Cycle and Rabbi Zeira's Statement
The Hybrid Status: One Drop of Blood Makes a Woman a Zavah Gedolah with Seven Clean Days
Rav Pappa Challenges Rava; Rava Initially Resists but Then Accepts Rabbi Zeira's Rule
Dam Chimud: Concern of Spotting Blood When Propositioned for Marriage
Contemporary Irrelevance of Dam Chimud and Its Halachic Implications
Practice of Going to Mikvah Only Within Four Days Before Wedding Night
Non-Jewish Practice of Chattan and Kallah Not Seeing Each Other Before Wedding

macro_segments.title: Chafifah: Hair Washing Before Immersion and Proper Cleansing Methods

micro_segments.title: Rava's Rule: Woman Should Not Wash Hair with Neser or Chol That Cause Problems
Chafifah as Rabbinic Requirement: Washing and Combing Hair to Remove Obstructions
Amemar in Name of Rava: Hair Must Be Washed in Hot Water, Not Cold
Reasons for Hot Water: Causes Hair to Be Pliable and Removes Dirt

macro_segments.title: Body Cleansing and Removing Chatzitzot from Folds and Hidden Areas

micro_segments.title: Rava: Woman Must Rinse Folds of Body with Water Before Mikvah
Distinction Between Beit Hakematzim (Folds) and Beit Hastamim (Hidden Places)
Water Not Required to Reach Hidden Places but Must Have Capacity; Principle of Rauy Levilah
Question: Must Entire Body Be Washed or Only Hair? Rishonim Debate

macro_segments.title: Chatzitzah in Inner Body Parts: Stories and Halachic Status

micro_segments.title: Story of Rebbe's Maidservant: Bone Found Between Teeth Requires New Tevilah
Rava: Finding Chatzitzah After Tevilah Requires New Tevilah Depending on Timing
If Chafifah Done Right Before Mikvah, No Need to Repeat; Otherwise Must Repeat
Alternative View: Same Day Rule Instead of Immediately Before

macro_segments.title: Practical Concerns and Restrictions for Mikvah Immersion

micro_segments.title: Rava: Woman Should Not Stand on Pottery Vessels in Mikvah
Rav Kahana's Reason: Fear of Making It Feel Like Bathhouse
Rav Shaman bar Abba: Woman Should Not Go to Mikvah in a Port
Rav's Father Made Mikvaot in Nisan and Mats in Tishrei for Daughters
Issue of Rainwater in Rivers: Cannot Use When Majority is Not Natural Running Water

macro_segments.title: Food Handling and Chatzitzah Risk Before Immersion

micro_segments.title: Rav Gidel in Name of Rav: Woman Who Gave Cooked Food to Son Cannot Assume Tevilah Good
Risk of Food Remaining on Body Even If Not Visible After Inspection

macro_segments.title: Specific Chatzitzot Issues: Scabs, Eye Discharge, Makeup, and Body Posture

micro_segments.title: Ami bar Abba: Scab Over Wound Not Chatzitzah Until Hard After Three Days
Mar Ukva: Eye Mucus When Moist Not Chatzitzah; When Dry and Greenish, It Is
Shmuel: Makeup Inside Eye Not Chatzitzah; On Outside Is; Exception If Eyes Fluttering
Rabbi Yochanan: Not to Open or Close Eyes Too Tightly During Immersion
Reish Lakish: Woman Must Go in Mikvah in Natural Body Posture, Not Artificially Extended
Analogy to Negaim: Determining Naturally Exposed Versus Folded Areas Based on Typical Activities

macro_segments.title: Chatzitzah and the Principle of Rov and Makpid

micro_segments.title: Rabbah bar bar Chana: One Hair Knotted on Itself Is Chatzitzah; Three Hairs Not
Rabbi Yochanan: Only One Hair Knotted Is Problem; Two Hairs Uncertain
Rav Yitzchak: Biblically, Chatzitzah Requires Majority of Body and Makpid (You Don't Want It)
Rabbinic Decree: Majority Without Makpid Also Problematic to Prevent Moving to Problematic Case
Exception: Minority Without Makpid Not Problem; Gemara Rejects Additional Safeguard
Definition of Makpid: Things You Want On Your Body Versus Things Ideally You Don't Want
Applications: Medical Devices, Casts, Braces, Nail Polish and Makpid Status

macro_segments.title: Hair-Specific Issues and Rov in Context of Braids and Hair Dye

micro_segments.title: Uncertainty: Whether Hair Is Evaluated by Rov of Body or Rov of Hair Itself
Examples: Braids and Hair Dye; Dye Absorbed Into Hair and Usually Permissible

macro_segments.title: Timing of Tevilah: Niddah Must Go at Night; When Woman Goes After Day Seven

micro_segments.title: Rav: Niddah Bt'zmanah (In Her Time) Can Only Immerse at Night
Rav: Shelo Bt'zmanah (Late) Can Go Day or Night
Rabbi Yochanan: Whether On Time or Late, Only at Night Due to Serach Bita (Daughters Following)
Concern: Daughters Will Remember and Imitate Mother's Practice, Leading to Day Seven Immersion

macro_segments.title: Takkanot for Day Immersion in Extenuating Circumstances

micro_segments.title: Rav Idi in Narash: Allowed Day Eight Immersion Because of Lions at Night
Rav Acha bar Yaakov in Papunya: Allowed Day Eight Due to Robbers at Night
Rav Yehuda in Pumbedita: Allowed Day Immersion Due to Cold at Night
Rava in Mechuza: Allowed Day Immersion Due to City Guard Restrictions

macro_segments.title: Rav Pappa's Universalization of Zavah Status and Its Implications for Tevilah Timing

micro_segments.title: Rav Pappa: All Women Now Treated as Safek Zavot Rabbinically Due to Zeira's Chumra
Zavah Can Immerse Day Seven of Clean Days Using Miktzat Hayom Ke-Kulo
Concern Based on Rabbi Shimon: Tevilah Day Seven Might Lead to Intercourse and Retroactive Bleeding
Contemporary Practice: Women Go Night of Day Seven Despite Zavah Status Because of Concern
Modern Question: Can Day Seven Immersion Be Permitted If No Risk of Intercourse Until Nightfall?

topical_tags.term: Safek Zavot (All Women as Possible Zavot)
Miktzat Hayom Ke-Kulo (Day Counts as Whole)
